name: Túria Natural Trail
description: Enjoy an easy 20.4-mile mountain biking route along the Túria Natural Trail, passing through diverse landscapes and historical features.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Quart de Poblet offer diverse terrain, from the flat, accessible paths of the Turia River Natural Park to more challenging routes in the wider Valencia region. The area is characterized by Mediterranean forests, Aleppo pines, and traditional agricultural lands, providing a green corridor for cycling. Historical hydraulic features, such as the Mislata irrigation canal, also form part of the unique landscape. Riders can expect varied conditions, including paved sections, gravel paths, and uneven terrain with small hills, rocks, and roots. Best mountain bike trails around Quart De Poblet The most popular mountain bike trail is Túria Natural Trail, a 20.4 miles (32.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Turia River, offering accessible paths through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Turia River Weir – Old Bridge over the Turia River loop from Paterna, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route explores the river's historical features and varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierra Perenxiza summit loop from Xirivella - Alqueries, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with elevation changes, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Quart De Poblet is defined by the Turia River Natural Park, Mediterranean forests, and historical irrigation canals. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat river paths to more challenging routes with uneven terrain. The routes in Quart De Poblet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 7800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Quart De Poblet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Quart De Poblet?
A: There are over 380 mountain bike trails around Quart De Poblet listed on komoot. These include a wide range of options, with 107 easy routes, 217 moderate routes, and 58 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Quart De Poblet?
A: The terrain around Quart De Poblet is diverse. You'll find mostly flat, accessible paths along the Turia River Natural Park, which are ideal for all fitness levels. Further afield, especially in the wider Valencia region, routes can feature uneven terrain with small hills, dirt roads, tracks, and even steep sections with rocks and roots for more advanced riders. The landscape includes Mediterranean forests, Aleppo pines, and traditional agricultural lands.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Quart De Poblet?
A: Yes, the paths along the Turia River Natural Park are generally flat and accessible, making them suitable for family outings. These routes often follow the riverbed, which has been transformed into a sprawling linear garden, providing extensive paths ideal for cycling. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916177660/turia-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Túria Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Quart De Poblet are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia River Weir – Old Bridge over the Turia River loop from Paterna&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 53.7 km circular path exploring historical features, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34587419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Perenxiza summit loop from Xirivella - Alqueries&lt;/a&gt; provides a more challenging loop with elevation changes.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the trails?
A: The trails offer access to various natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can explore the Turia River Natural Park itself, with its diverse ecosystems. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass near highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2674584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carasoles Geodetic Point&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1383092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto Antennas – View of Valencia&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1338494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Perenxisa Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, some routes can lead you to impressive natural features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4058748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Galleç Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight. You can also explore areas with gorges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4254465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de l'Horteta&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carraixet Ravine – Pont Sec Area&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique landscapes for mountain biking.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Quart De Poblet?
A: The mountain bike trails around Quart De Poblet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 1100 reviews. More than 7800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the accessible paths along the Turia River and the diverse natural landscapes.

Q: Is it possible to use public transport with bikes to access the trails?
A: Quart de Poblet is well-connected within the Valencia region. While specific rules for bikes on public transport can vary, the area's strategic location and access to the Turia River Natural Park make it relatively easy to reach. It's advisable to check local public transport regulations regarding bicycle carriage before planning your trip.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Quart De Poblet?
A: Given Quart de Poblet's role as a gateway to the Turia River Natural Park, there are typically parking options available in and around the town, especially near popular access points to the park. Many routes start from urban areas or trailheads where parking is often provided or easily found.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Quart De Poblet?
A: The Valencia region generally enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate, making mountain biking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are usually mild, allowing for year-round activity, though occasional rain might affect trail conditions.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Many natural areas, including parts of the Turia River Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly, especially on trails where dogs can be kept on a leash. It's always best to check specific park regulations or signage along individual routes to ensure dogs are permitted and to be mindful of other trail users and wildlife.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area boasts a rich historical hydraulic heritage. You might encounter structures like the Mislata irrigation canal, an original section of which is a Site of Cultural Interest. The Turia River also features ancient bridges and historical water partition systems like the 'Llengües de Sant Onofre', adding a cultural dimension to your ride.
tips: Beautiful road and in excellent condition that runs along the Turia River for about 16 km connecting Riba Roja del Turia and Quart de Poblet. It can be done both on mtb and gravel or also on foot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for mountain biking on the Túria Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies significantly. You'll find conditioned paths, including paved and gravel roads, especially in the more urbanized sections near Valencia city. As you move into the natural park, expect more rugged dirt roads and tracks. Some advanced sections, particularly in the Alto Turia region, can be very rocky and dry with loose stone, and there are mentions of single tracks, offering a mix of wider paths and narrower, technical routes.

faqs.question: Is the Túria Natural Trail suitable for beginners and families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail system offers routes for all skill levels. There are many easy, circular paths ideal for families and children, with minimal elevation gain. These sections, like those within the Turia Riverbed park in Valencia city, are typically well-conditioned for bikes. More challenging routes are also available for experienced riders.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery, from mountainous areas to coastal plains. You'll pass through the Mediterranean forest landscape of the Turia River Natural Park, observing flora and fauna like squirrels, herons, and trout. The route also features traditional meadowlands and historical elements such as dry stone architecture. In urban sections, the transformed Turia Riverbed park provides a unique 'green corridor' experience, passing landmarks like the City of Arts and Sciences.

faqs.question: What are the main access points and parking options for the trail?
faqs.answer: The Túria Natural Trail has multiple access points, making it convenient to join. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, the trail's accessibility, particularly in urban areas like Valencia city, suggests various public parking options would be available nearby. For sections within the natural park, look for designated parking areas near trailheads.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Parc Natural del Túria?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or permits required to access the Parc Natural del Túria or to mountain bike on the Túria Natural Trail. The park is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Túria Natural Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on the Túria Natural Trail, especially in the natural park sections. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Be mindful of local regulations, particularly in more urban or sensitive ecological areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Túria Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike the Túria Natural Trail is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

faqs.question: What notable features or highlights can I see along the Túria Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in ecological and cultural value. A significant highlight is the Turia Eco Route and Riverside Trail, which showcases the region's diverse Mediterranean flora and fauna. You'll also experience the unique transformation of the former Turia Riverbed in Valencia city into a sprawling park, offering a distinct cycling experience through a 'green law' area.

faqs.question: Does the Túria Natural Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route extensively follows the Camí natural del Túria (94% of the route) and also passes through significant sections of El Camí IT5TR7 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (74%). You may also encounter shorter segments of the Anell Verd Metropolità de València and the Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Valencia.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or rules to be aware of when mountain biking in the Parc Natural del Túria?
faqs.answer: When mountain biking in the Parc Natural del Túria, it's important to stay on marked trails to protect the natural environment. Respect wildlife, carry out all trash, and be considerate of other trail users, including hikers and equestrians. While no specific permits are needed, adhering to general park etiquette helps preserve the area for everyone.

waypoints.name: Turia Eco Route and Riverside Trail
